scopeNote: Ran the configured milestone validators and reviewed all completed implementation features in the journal-and-account-core milestone. The scoped test suite, compile/typecheck, and Spotless lint check all passed, but scrutiny found two blocking feature-level issues, so the milestone cannot advance to user testing yet.

blockingIssues.description: AccountCatalogService trims account codes but still persists original case while relying on a case-sensitive unique key, so concurrent requests can create case-variant duplicates such as 'cash' and 'CASH'. That breaks the promised company-scoped duplicate-code conflict behavior and leaves downstream ignore-case account resolution ambiguous. See .factory/validation/journal-and-account-core/scrutiny/reviews/chart-of-accounts-and-default-account-hardcut.json.

A live competing GST truth owner remains at /api/v1/reports/gst-return because ReportService.gstReturn() still rebuilds GST totals from invoice and purchase tables instead of consuming the canonical accounting owner introduced by the feature. That leaves report-side GST math active in the corridor that was meant to be hard-cut. See .factory/validation/journal-and-account-core/scrutiny/reviews/journal-posting-and-core-read-model-hardcut.json.

appliedUpdates.description: Added `targeted-gl-core-proof` so future GL-core workers and validators can run the narrow chart/default-account ownership and duplicate-handling proof suite from the shared service catalog instead of relying on ad hoc Maven selectors.
Recorded the first-round scrutiny synthesis for the journal-and-account-core milestone, including validator results, per-feature review outcomes, and blocking findings.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the accounting module guidance to document AccountResolutionOwnerService as the controller-facing canonical owner for chart-of-accounts and default-account API resolution, instead of implying AccountingService alone owns that boundary.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The passing implementation introduced AccountResolutionOwnerService as the explicit owner used by AccountController, but the module guidance still routes workers toward AccountingService as the only accounting-host entrypoint for this corridor. The discrepancy was noted in the review for chart-of-accounts-and-default-account-hardcut.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require workers to include the skillFeedback object consistently in final handoffs so scrutiny can verify whether any feature intentionally deviated from the worker skill procedure.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The review for journal-posting-and-core-read-model-hardcut found that its handoff omitted skillFeedback entirely, unlike adjacent accounting hard-cut handoffs, making it harder to verify whether the surviving legacy /reports/gst-return path reflected an intentional scoped exception or a missed retirement step.
